title: How to Watch All Spider-Man Movies in Order Before Brand New Day (2026 Guide)

author: StarScoop Daily Staff
imageAlt: Spider-Man films watch guide — how to stream every Spider-Man movie before Brand New Day 2026
metaDescription: How to watch all Spider-Man movies in order before Brand New Day on July 31, 2026 — the essential MCU watch order, where to stream each film, and what you must see first.
content:
One Week Until Brand New Day — Here's How to Catch Up

Spider-Man: Brand New Day swings into cinemas on July 31, 2026 — and if you haven't caught up on the Spider-Man back catalogue yet, you have exactly one week. Don't panic. Not all nine films are required viewing, and the ones that are can be streamed right now without leaving the sofa.

Here's the full guide: which films to watch, in what order, where to find them, and — crucially — which single film is non-negotiable before you buy a ticket to Brand New Day.

What Do You Actually Need to Watch Before Brand New Day?

The direct answer: the MCU Spider-Man trilogy. Everything else is optional background that will enrich your experience — but the three Tom Holland-led MCU films are the essential watch path for Brand New Day.

Brand New Day picks up directly from the ending of Spider-Man: No Way Home (2021). If you haven't seen that film, the entire premise of Brand New Day — Peter Parker's anonymity, the erased memory of everyone who knew him, the emotional weight of starting from zero — will land without context. No Way Home is not optional.

Spider-Man: Homecoming (2017) and Spider-Man: Far From Home (2019) build the relationships and character development that make No Way Home's ending hit so hard. If you have the time, watch all three in order. If you only have time for one — skip straight to No Way Home. More on that below.

The Essential MCU Trilogy — In Order

1. Spider-Man: Homecoming (2017) — Tom Holland's debut as the solo MCU Spider-Man. Peter Parker is 15, eager to prove himself to Tony Stark, and figuring out what kind of hero he actually wants to be. Sets up every relationship that pays off in the later films — including MJ, Ned, and Aunt May. Watch time: 2h 13m.

2. Spider-Man: Far From Home (2019) — Set immediately after Avengers: Endgame, Peter is grieving Tony Stark's death and just wants a normal school trip to Europe. Nothing goes normally. A key villain is introduced, and the film ends with a cliffhanger that sets the stage for No Way Home's entire premise. Watch time: 2h 9m.

3. Spider-Man: No Way Home (2021) — The essential film. The multiverse opens, consequences spiral out of control, and the ending is one of the most emotionally devastating conclusions in superhero cinema history. Brand New Day begins exactly where this one ends. Watch time: 2h 28m. Do not skip this one.

The Full Nine-Film Watch Order (If You Want It All)

If you want the complete Spider-Man film experience before July 31, here's every film in release order:

The Raimi Trilogy (2002–2007): Spider-Man (2002), Spider-Man 2 (2004), Spider-Man 3 (2007) — starring Tobey Maguire. Sam Raimi's films defined the modern superhero movie. They're not required for Brand New Day, but Spider-Man 2 in particular remains one of the best superhero films ever made. Total watch time: ~6.5 hours.

The Amazing Spider-Man Series (2012–2014): The Amazing Spider-Man (2012) and The Amazing Spider-Man 2 (2014) — starring Andrew Garfield. A grittier, more emotionally raw take on Peter Parker. The Gwen Stacy storyline across these two films remains heartbreaking. Again — not required for Brand New Day, but rewarding. Total watch time: ~4.5 hours.

The MCU Trilogy + Brand New Day (2017–2026): The four Tom Holland films are the direct through-line. These are the essential watches. Total watch time for the three existing films: ~6.5 hours.

Where to Stream Every Spider-Man Film in 2026

All three MCU Spider-Man films — Homecoming, Far From Home, and No Way Home — are streaming on Disney+ right now. This is your one-stop destination for Brand New Day prep. A single Disney+ subscription covers everything you need.

The Tobey Maguire Raimi trilogy is also available on Disney+ in most regions following the extended Sony-Disney streaming agreement. In markets where they're not included, all three are available to rent or purchase on Prime Video, Apple TV, and Google Play Movies.

The Andrew Garfield Amazing Spider-Man films are streaming on Disney+ in many markets, and available to rent on all major digital platforms elsewhere. Search by title on your preferred service to confirm availability in your region.

Spider-Man: Brand New Day will be in cinemas exclusively from July 31. Based on Marvel's recent streaming release pattern, expect it on Disney+ approximately 45 days after its theatrical debut — around mid-September 2026.

If You Only Have Time for One Film

Simple answer: watch Spider-Man: No Way Home on Disney+ tonight. It's 2 hours and 28 minutes. The final ten minutes are the direct setup for everything in Brand New Day — the memory spell, the sacrifice, the anonymous new life Peter chooses.

Understanding what Peter gave up — and why — is the emotional foundation of Brand New Day. If you walk into the cinema on July 31 having only seen No Way Home, you will understand everything that matters. Every other film on this list adds layers, but No Way Home is the one that's truly non-negotiable.

For everything else confirmed about the upcoming film, read our full preview of Spider-Man: Brand New Day — and check the latest early reactions in our Where to Watch section for more streaming guides.
